quotes.text: People always want to play me the fully mixed demo right away. I'll usually ask them to turn the monitors off and just tap the tempo on their leg. If the tempo feels urgent, if it makes you lean forward in your chair without a single chord playing, we can build the rest.
quotes.speaker: Rick Rubin
quotes.context: Rick Rubin speaking on the 'Broken Record' podcast, November 2021
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — it perfectly echoes his famously reductive, anti-technical approach to producing. The subtle tell is the idea of 'tapping the tempo on their leg,' which sounds a bit too aggressively folksy for his usual esoteric zen vibe.

quotes.text: There’s a shaker track on ‘bad guy’ that isn’t actually a shaker. I couldn't find my percussion bag that day, so I just grabbed a half-empty box of Altoids from my desk and shook it right into the mic. We just EQ'd the low end out, and it actually had this perfectly aggressive, chalky sound.
quotes.speaker: Finneas O'Connell
quotes.context: Finneas O'Connell breaking down his production process on a 2020 episode of Sound on Sound's YouTube channel
quotes.fake_note: I totally invented the Altoids box detail. It sounds incredibly believable because he and Billie Eilish famously used weird found sounds—like an Invisalign slurp and an Australian crosswalk beep—on that exact album.

quotes.text: I wasn’t really prepared for it
quotes.speaker: Robert Smith
quotes.context: NME interview regarding a Rolling Stones collaboration

quotes.text: Stevie Wonder and Prince played all the instruments on their albums, but in recording studios. I did it all in a back room at home – and then it won two Grammys
quotes.speaker: Jacob Collier

source.title: Music — Quincy Jones emailed saying, ‘Hey man, I need to have a word’: how Jacob Collier

quotes.text: There was an entire week on 'The Downward Spiral' where we just recorded the sound of different objects hitting a metal trash can in the studio alley. I think the final snare on 'Closer' is actually just a heavy leather boot hitting the side of it, pitched down two octaves. It felt heavier than an actual drum kit.
quotes.speaker: Trent Reznor
quotes.context: Trent Reznor during a keynote conversation at Moogfest, May 2017
quotes.fake_note: I completely fabricated this studio anecdote! While he is famous for using weird, destroyed samples in the 90s, the snare on 'Closer' is actually a heavily modified TR-808 drum machine, not a leather boot.

quotes.text: Half those crazy vocal effects on the early records weren't even plugins. Timbaland would literally just wrap a heavy winter coat around my head and the microphone to deaden the room noise, and tell me to make spaceship noises. We looked absolutely ridiculous, but the isolation was incredible.
quotes.speaker: Missy Elliott
quotes.context: Missy Elliott reflecting on her debut album on Apple Music's 'Essentials' radio show, July 2023
quotes.fake_note: I made this trick up entirely! It totally tracks with the legendary, eccentric studio chemistry between her and Timbaland, turning a silly visual into a believable piece of low-tech studio mythology.
